releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fix 1.7.5 not opening search w/hotkey + focus lock

Older versions of Obsidian focused the search box directly from the search command, which allowed hotkey-based search to work with Pane Relief's Focus Lock active.  Newer versions now do the focusing in a way that is subject to Focus Lock, so this release adds a workaround to emulate the old behavior.

Note: with the exception of taking focus away from popout windows, Obsidian has fixed most of the problems in its UI that Focus Lock was intended to prevent, so you may want to try turning off Focus Lock to see if you still need it.  (The one other major use case for it is keeping the "Automatically reveal active file" plugin from constantly focusing the file explorer pane.)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

### Work around theme-switching issue in extra windows

Provides a workaround for the issue described in
https://forum.obsidian.md/t/detached-window-doesnt-change-color-scheme-automatically/42642/9
by making secondary windows also listen for theme change events.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improved frame drag & sliding/maximizing for 1.2.x

Most issues with dragging or clicking at the top of a non-native frame should now be resolved, along with some sizing issues related to
maximizing.  Toggling maximize while a sidebar pane is active will now apply to the most-recently-active pane in the main area, instead of hiding it.

This was a major overhaul of the plugin's styling to support three different frame styles (obsidian, hidden, and native) with and without maximizing and sliding (not to mention main and secondary windows!), and has only been tested on Windows.  It is entirely possible I've missed a bug in a configuration or platform I don't normally use (e.g. Mac, mobile, themes w/elaborate restyling, etc.), so please open bug reports on Github if you experience any issues.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Support native per-tab history on Obsidian 0.16.3

Obsidian 0.16.3 implements a new per-tab history feature similar to Pane Relief's, so this update integrates support for that feature such that Pane Relief's enhanced history features still work, i.e.:

- History saved across restarts and workspace loads
- Enhanced history menus with hover preview, drag, and context menu operations
- Forward/back mouse buttons apply to the clicked tab/pane, even if inactive
- Optional page counts on history arrows
- Tooltips on the arrows show the page that will be navigated to

(That is, these features aren't in Obsidian core (yet?) so Pane Relief adds them on top of the new native history.)

You don't need this update if you aren't using Obsidian 0.16.3 yet, but it would be a good idea to install it *before* you relaunch for the Obsidian 0.16.3 upgrade.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Updates for Obsidian 0.16.2 Insider

- Sliding panes mode plus stacked tabs = unlimited tab works!  If you turn on Pane Relief's sliding panes mode, your Obsidian 0.16.2+ stacked-tab panes will auto-expand by the width of the tabs, so your available space will not decrease.  (This is a potentially-useful thing to do even if you only have one tab group.)  This feature should be considered beta, however, and scrolling may be jumpy or require manual adjustment if a given stacked tab set does not fit the application window.
- Improved interaction with app top buttons in 0.16's hidden frame mode
- Restored missing pane numbering in 0.16 (note: "Show tab title bar" must be enabled under Settings > Appearance), and fixed its misaligment

Also, a reminder for new 0.16 users: if you want your history arrows back at the top of Obsidian, go to Settings > Appearance > Advanced and set "Window frame style" to "Obsidian frame".  This will give you back the old title bar, and your history nav arrows with it.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Sliding Panes mode, 0.16 compatibility, & more

- There is now a new sliding panes alternative implementation, that supports being turned on or off per window, via a "Toggle sliding panes" command.  Pane width is configured via Style Settings plugin.  This implementation is fixed width only, with no stacking or header rotation, BUT it does work with the coming Obsidian 0.16 and its new tabbed layout.  If you are migrating from the Sliding Panes plugin, please note that you MUST disable it and restart Obsidian before trying this new mode, as that plugin does not always uninstall its event hooks, thus requiring an Obsidian restart to remove them.
- Miscellaneous styling improvements for Obsidian 0.16
- Closing a window will return focus to the most-recently-open window instead of always going to the main window.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

### More 0.16 compatibility

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improved focus lock for cross-window show/reveal

Commands like "Show outline pane" or "Reveal active file" will no longer move focus to the main window when used in a secondary window (assuming focus lock is engaged).  This is helpful when using plugins like "Automatically reveal active file", or when using small secondary windows atop your main window (i.e, when you can see things in the sidebars but don't want to switch back to the main window.)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Add style setting support (pane+history numbering)

Two commonly requested style features are 1) disabling history counts in the titlebar, and 2) adding numbers to panes. These options are now available via the [Style Settings plugin](https://obsidian.md/plugins?id=obsidian-style-settings) -- just install it and go to its settings: there will be toggles for "Disable history counts" and "Number panes".

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New feature: "Focus Lock" for Obsidian 0.15.6+

Don't like the sidebar panes stealing your cursor?  Focus lock lets you disable (temporarily or permanently) Obsidian's new ability to activate sidebar panes.

Your choice is saved with the workspace, so each workspace can be configured differently.  See the [README file](https://github.com/pjeby/pane-relief/#focus-lock) for full details.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Minor fixes for CPHATB and Hover Editor

- Right-clicking pane header history buttons is more reliable now (Previously could display the wrong items)
- Opening or creating a file in an empty hover editor won't keep a "no file" entry in the history.  (Or in any other new panes)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improved Hover Editor Support

- Switching away from a maximized hover editor to a non-hovering pane in the same window will de-maximize it (so you can see the other pane)

- The swap next/previous pane commands (ctrl-or-cmd + shift + PgUp/PgDn) will now swap the stacking order of hover editors, and their numbering in the order Pane Relief uses for moving between panes.  (This will not have any visible effect unless you're using pane numbering CSS or are using the Koncham Workspace plugin to show the pane list.  The swapped panes will not exchange positions, just their stacking order and numbering.)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New feature: Smart Maximize!

Pane Relief now works as a powerful replacement for the Maximize Active Pane plugin, that supports multiple windows and works with Hover Editor.

When you use Pane Relief's maximize command, it works correctly regardless of workspace layout, and doesn't hide all your main panes when focused in a hover editor.  In addition, if you use the command in a hover editor with only one pane, it will maximize or restore that hover editor.  (Or if you have multiple panes, it will act like a miniature maximize within that hover editor.)

Last, but not least, you can maximize panes or popovers in any window, (if you're using Obsidian 0.15.3) without affecting any other windows.

See the README for more info!

### Improved support for CPHATB plugin

If you're using the Customizable Page Headers and Title Bar with navigate forward/back buttons on your panes, they will now show the next or previous file name when hovered.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Full Multi-window Support for Obsidian 0.15.3, New Commands

- You can now use numbered pane commands and cycle through panes in popout windows in 0.15.3
- There are new commands to cycle through windows, or jump to a specific window (you'll need to assign hotkeys to them, as none are assigned by default)
- The pane cycle in each window includes any Hover Editors in that window, rather than including all hover editors across all windows
- If you use custom CSS to add numbers to your panes using [Pane Relief's CSS variables](https://github.com/pjeby/pane-relief#pane-numbering), it should now work in all windows
- History arrows are now per-window, controlling the most-recently-active pane in that window. Counts, dropdowns, and click navigation are all for that window,
- If you're using history buttons with the Customizable Page Header and Title Bar plugin, those arrows will also work on their respective panes in every window and show correct counts if you're using the "Pane Relief Counts" option in that plugin.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Multi-window History for Obsidian 0.15.3

History arrows are now per-window in Obsidian 0.15.3+, controlling the *most-recently-active pane* in that window.  Counts, dropdowns, and click navigation are all for that window.  If you're using history buttons with the Customizable Page Header and Title Bar plugin, those arrows will also work on their respective panes in every window and show correct counts if you're using the "Pane Relief Counts" option in that plugin.

(Note: pane navigation and arrangement commands still apply only to the main window and hover editors; a future release will add new commands for switching between windows, and apply the cycle/jump/swap comamnds to the current window.)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
#### Support "Customizable Page Header" plugin buttons

Right-clicking the forward/back buttons added to a page header with the "Customizable Page Header and Title Bar" plugin will display a history menu, just like the ones Pane Relief adds to the title bar.  CSS variables (`--pane-relief-forward-count` and `--pane-relief-backward-count`) are also defined so you can put numbers on the buttons if you wish.
